calendar.matters:
DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jan-30-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Lawrence D. Miller, Law Offices of Lawrence D. Miller, Tel. 650.592.9151, for plaintiffs;
Ray Alexander, of Law Offices of Raymond M. Alexander, Jr., Tel. 415.989.9011, for defendant Tristan You.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT TRISTAN YOU's DEMURRER is sustained without leave to amend as to both causes of action. Both causes of action in the first amended complaint stem from Daniel S. Lee's alleged transfer of approximately $104,850 from June 10, 2013 to November 18, 2013 to Northeastern University for the benefit of Mr. You. (FAC, 12.) In light of the absence of authority on whether creditors may attack college tuition payments by a parent alleged to have made fraudulent transfers under the Uniform Voidable Transfers Act, the court adopts the reasoning set forth in In re Palladino (Bankr. D. Mass 2016) 556 B.R. 10, 16, stating "[a] parent can reasonably assume that paying for a child to obtain an undergraduate degree will enhance the financial well-being of the child which in turn will confer an economic benefit on the parent." Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, Email: geales@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Dec-19-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT TRISTAN YOU's DEMURRER to First Amended Complaint is OFF CALENDAR. The moving party failed to file a meet and confer declaration in compliance with CCP 430.41. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/ru
